behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three collectors -> aggregation/payload builder -> summary step input. A unit test of one formatter can miss wiring/field-drop bugs across modules (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network behavior is required to reproduce this specific failure; source clients can be deterministic fakes (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System because behavior text is about "content passed" completeness, not real endpoint/network semantics. Rejected Agentic because it validates pre-summary input composition, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with three stub source adapters returning distinct sentinel values, then assert the exact payload sent into summary generation contains all three source sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation/fallback behavior (source adapter failure -> report composer), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). It does not require real network faults; a stubbed source throwing the same exception class can trigger the path (REAL-INFRA no, MOCK-HIDE no for this behavior). Rejected System because behavior text focuses on graceful degradation output ("data unavailable" note) rather than transport-level timeout/socket behavior. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real report orchestration modules with one failing source stub and two successful stubs; assert run completes, output includes the unavailable marker for only the failed section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated narrative faithfulness to input values, which is model-output quality/judgment (LLM-DEP yes). Deterministic stubs would always pass and hide hallucination/omission/misstatement failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow because behavior describes one model-output quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence (MULTI-STEP no).
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model with representative inputs containing critical numeric/key facts; evaluate whether produced narrative preserves those facts (allowing wording variance) across repeated runs with pass/fail thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external APIs/email transport and inbox delivery, which require real HTTP/service behavior (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process fakes can hide SMTP/API auth, provider acceptance, and mailbox delivery issues (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Agentic: although pipeline may include summary generation, this behavior text is specifically about end-to-end delivery to inbox, not semantic model-output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end against real/staging source endpoints and real email provider test account; verify one scheduled execution produces an email in the configured recipient inbox with expected report artifact/metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff across runs: send failure -> local persistence -> later reload/retry. This is multi-module + local filesystem persistence, fitting Integration. Real network outage semantics are not required for this behavior; a mail adapter stub that raises unreachable errors is sufficient to test fallback/retry logic (REAL-INFRA no for minimum). Rejected System because behavior text can be satisfied by invoking two runs with persisted local state, without validating real scheduler/transport quirks. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-judgment dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use real persistence layer (temp filesystem), stub mail sender to fail on run 1 and succeed on run 2; assert report file is written after failure, consumed on next invocation, and delivery retried/cleared correctly.
